title: Correct logic for demux'ing channels
body:
* Move check for requesting output into case statement

reference #6574 

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jwhonce* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@mheon This commit fixes demux'ing the output.  But now messages from conmon appear to be exposed...

```
$ bin/podman-remote --url $MYSOCK run --name foo1 -d alpine top
f5c4cf63f2e206aa99a12b625c72fa9de4b740e6f02b493abe3eb35f03c3cc11
$ bin/podman-remote --url $MYSOCK exec foo1 date
[conmon:d]: exec with attach is waiting for start message from parent
[conmon:d]: exec with attach got start message from parent
```

Damn - took me a second to realize, but yeah, this is definitely the fix.

LGTM
@jwhonce Hmmm. When I was testing Exec, those were fd=3...
Do you know what stream they're coming in on?
@jwhonce Even worse, it's not showing the output of `date` unless I provide `-i`

The `conmon` output only happens with `--log-level=debug` is specified on the server, so I don't know if printing Conmon debug is the worst thing. Not printing the output of `date` is a serious bug though.
We're getting an early EOF/dropped connection out of Conmon. Maybe we're not draining the buffer entirely in the exec case?
@jwhonce IMO, we should just merge this, file a bug for the further issue and we'll look into it later.
Please fix the commit message.  This will not work well when someone goes back to look at history in git.
